one_liner: CRCL +3.3% to $76 on Nomura FX settlement partnership; VCP detected but stock is -29% from $105 IPO-era high with RSI 36 still in downtrend territory.

price_action.intraday_story: CRCL dipped to $72.39 (testing June 26 support) then recovered to close at $75.96 near the high. Low volume (0.65x) on the recovery is mixed — not enough conviction to call a bottom, but the support test held.

news_catalyst.headline: Circle + Nomura Partner on Stablecoin FX Settlement for Japanese Corporations
news_catalyst.summary: Circle Internet Group revealed a planned FX settlement tie-up with Nomura for Japanese corporate clients using USDC stablecoins for yen conversions and cross-border payments, targeting 2027 deployment. The Nomura partnership drove a 6.92% close on June 26. Japan's FX settlement market is massive and stablecoin regulatory clarity in Japan makes USDC well-positioned. However, CRCL has declined from $105+ in early June to $76 today — the Nomura news is positive but hasn't reversed the broader downtrend.

technical.summary: VCP detected + Accumulation Phase B + RSI 36 approaching oversold. The trifecta of technical signals points to a base-building setup, but the stock is still 29% below EMA50 ($91.41) and EMA200 ($100.72). Low-volume support hold is constructive but needs a catalyst to trigger the breakout.

wyckoff_narrative: Phase B accumulation with VCP detected in the $64–$76 range. The $72.39 today is the secondary test of the prior swing low. If volume dries up further over the next 2–3 sessions while price holds $72+, that's the LPS setup for a VCP breakout attempt above the $76 pivot.

valuation.summary: DCF not applicable to CRCL — the company's value derives from USDC in circulation (interest income on reserves) and the stablecoin ecosystem optionality. Bull case at $340 per some analysts; current $76 is deep in the bear scenario for a company with the Nomura and global enterprise partnerships.

key_strengths: Nomura partnership positions USDC in Japan's massive corporate FX market
USDC is the #2 stablecoin with regulatory clarity advantages over USDT
key_concerns: Grade D fundamentals reflect pre-profitability crypto-adjacent business model
-29% from recent high — institutional distribution ongoing
Stablecoin regulatory framework still evolving globally
fundamentals.summary: Grade D reflects early-stage economics. The Nomura partnership is genuinely strategic but 2027 revenue is speculative at this stage.

risks: EMA50 at $91 and EMA200 at $100 are both 20–32% overhead — full recovery requires sustained catalyst flow
Stablecoin regulatory surprise (SEC action, GENIUS Act amendment) could gap the stock lower
VCP pivot undefined — pattern hasn't completed enough contractions to identify the exact breakout level
